name: Team Kasich
subject: Release: Gov. Kasich Washington Post Op-Ed: Loose Talk About War With North Korea is Irresponsible
body:
In his latest Washington Post op-ed, Gov. John Kasich makes the
case that the United States should end the war of words and talk
of war with North Korea without exhausting other proven options
to secure peace.

FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE

December 26, 2017
Contact: Press@johnkasich.com

In his latest Washington Post
op-ed, Gov. John Kasich makes the case that the United States
should end the war of words and talk of war with North Korea
without exhausting other proven options to secure peace.

Gov. Kasich served for eighteen
years on the U.S. House Armed Services Committee. He was a key
supporter of President Ronald Reagan's national security policies
and played a role in every major national security effort that
helped end the Cold War. He then went to work helping transform
our military into a flexible force able to respond to more varied
threats, like those we face today.

Loose Talk About War With North
Korea is Irresponsible

"With tensions continuing to
build between the United States and North Korea, there's growing
talk by politicians and TV pundits that we are on the brink of
war. In truth, we shouldn't be anywhere close.

This increasingly hot war of
words - including loose talk about the probability of war - does
nothing to bring us closer to where we need to be on North Korea,
especially when military options short of war remain on the
table. In fact, with millions of lives at stake, waging a war of
words is a distraction from the serious task at hand. Any kind of
war - especially nuclear war - should not be an option until all
other options are exhausted. And, in the case of North Korea,
there are several roads not yet taken.

First, North Korea is not, as
some claim, "sanctioned-out." We are nowhere near to applying the
same type of restraints on North Korea that were successful in
bringing Iran to the negotiating table.

In fact, the breadth of sanctions
we have placed on North Korea to date are far less than what was
applied in earlier crises on Russia, Syria and Iran. While our
sanctions on North Korea have clearly escalated, we still have
the option to penalize and seize the assets of North Korea's
enablers in other countries that enable Pyongyang to evade the
full brunt of financial measures. We can expand our focus on
shipping and work with our allies to deny maritime insurance to
the vessels heading to or from North Korea. Last month, we
targeted sanctions on 20 such vessels. Many more North Korean
vessels are active and engaged in illicit activities beyond the
small number designated by the Treasury Department.

We also can do more to expose
those who use North Korean slave labor and to block any
remittances back to Kim Jong Un's regime.

Second, none of this will work
without more pressure to hold the reluctant Chinese government
accountable for the commitments it has made and to target more
Chinese entities that support the North Korean government. The
overwhelming majority of North Korea's trade - 90 percent - is
with or facilitated by China, and despite agreed-upon U.N.
sanctions, much of this economic activity continues. Actions
should include targeting a greater number of Chinese banks that
deal with North Korea, fining their U.S. subsidiaries and
freezing their U.S. assets.

This year, the international
banking transaction network, known as SWIFT, moved to prevent
North Korean banks from using the global messaging system to
facilitate international transactions, but that doesn't impact
Chinese banks that transact for the North Koreans. We should
consider expanding this ban to include Chinese banks with any
North Korean connections.

Finally, we need to ask: Where
are our allies on all of this? Instead of threatening a bilateral
war between the United States and the North Korea, we should be
working with allies - including South Korea and Japan - to
threaten increased multilateral pressure to choke the North
Korean regime. While economic sanctions have not proved to be
effective yet, they have not been fully exhausted and tested.
Part of the reason the previous administration succeeded in
bringing Iran to the table - regardless of the flaws of their
final deal - was due to internationally coordinated sanctions.
Thankfully, the U.N. Security Council has adopted three rounds of
such sanctions this year, including significant measures last
week.

With millions of lives hanging in
the balance, the last thing we need is to have politicians and
pundits predicting odds on the probability of war. It's neither
an accurate nor a helpful way to treat a complex international
challenge."

Read the original op-ed in the
Washington Post (here).

Paid for by Kasich
for America, not authorized by any candidate or candidate's
committee.
www.JohnKasich.com

This message was intended for: dpwillis67@gmail.com
You were added to the system July 18, 2015.

http://email.sermodigital.com/p/is9rs9PotN

name: Bob =?UTF-8?Q?Fertik=2C=20Democrats=2Ecom?=
subject: The FCC betrayed Net Neutrality - tell Congress to save the Open Internet
body:
Tell Congress: Save net neutrality (https://act.credoaction.com/sign/pass-CRA_dems)

The petition to Congress reads:
"Save net neutrality. Support the joint resolution of disapproval overturning the FCC's reversal of the 2015 Open Internet rules."

Add your name:

(https://act.credoaction.com/sign/pass-CRA_dems)

Dear Activist,

(https://act.credoaction.com/sign/pass-CRA_dems)

Trump's Federal Communications Commission (FCC) recently voted to kill the open internet. They ignored millions of Americans to end the internet as we know it and give big cable companies control of what we say and do online.

But this fight isn't over. Congress has the authority to overturn the FCC's vote. Congress can use a "resolution of disapproval" to reverse this disastrous decision and restore the rules that protect the open internet.1 In both the House and Senate, members of Congress are introducing these resolutions – now we need to get others to sign on.

Tell Congress: Save net neutrality. Support the resolution of disapproval overturning the FCC's repeal of net neutrality. Click here to sign the petition. (https://act.credoaction.com/sign/pass-CRA_dems)

Millions of Americans called on the FCC to keep the internet working the way it is now – instead of letting big cable companies control what all Americans see, say and do online. But Trump's FCC Chairman Ajit Pai ignored us, and on Dec. 14, the FCC rushed to kill net neutrality. The agency dismissed the fact that the public comment process was corrupted by millions of fake comments and ignored requests to delay or cancel the vote from 18 state attorneys general, Republican and Democratic members of Congress, the creator of the internet, and of course millions of people who depend on the internet every day.2

Congress has the power and the responsibility to right this wrong. Thanks to the Congressional Review Act, Congress can review new regulations – in this case, the FCC's Orwellian-sounding “Restoring Internet Freedom” rule – and overrule them by passing a joint resolution of disapproval.3 Republicans in Congress used the process earlier this year when they repealed important broadband privacy protections. And in the Senate, even the minority party can force a resolution of disapproval to a vote – making every senator go on the record for or against net neutrality.

Losing the open internet is particularly harmful for communities of color, who rely on the open internet to organize, create economic opportunity and fight back against oppression.4 More than 80 percent of Americans support net neutrality,5 and more than 1 million people called Congress in the last month alone, asking their representatives to save net neutrality.

Free speech and civil rights champions throughout history have faced setbacks, but they did not back down. The fight for a free and open internet is no different. We will continue to fight until we restore the rules that protect the internet as a platform for free speech, organizing, innovation and community building.

Trump's FCC chair has shown that he is in the pocket of big telecom companies. But unlike the FCC, members of Congress are elected and they must answer to us.

Tell Congress: Save net neutrality. Support the resolution of disapproval condemning the FCC's repeal of net neutrality. Click the link below to sign the petition:

https://act.credoaction.com/sign/pass-CRA_dems (https://act.credoaction.com/sign/pass-CRA_dems)

Thanks for fighting back,

Brandy Doyle, Campaign Manager
CREDO Action from Working Assets (http://www.credoaction.com)

Add your name:

(https://act.credoaction.com/sign/pass-CRA_dems)

References:

Kate Conger and Dell Cameron, "Wait, can Congress stop the FCC from trampling net neutrality? (https://gizmodo.com/wait-can-congress-stop-the-fcc-from-trampling-net-neut-1821292981) " Gizmodo, Dec. 14, 2017. Ted Johnson, "FCC repeals net neutrality rules amid protests, lawsuit threats (http://variety.com/2017/biz/news/net-neutrality-vote-fcc-preview-1202640501/) ," Variety, Dec. 14, 2017. Conger and Cameron, "Wait, can Congress stop the FCC from trampling net neutrality? (https://gizmodo.com/wait-can-congress-stop-the-fcc-from-trampling-net-neut-1821292981) " Timothy Karr, "Voices for Internet Freedom: FCC chairman's anti-net neutrality plan will harm people of color (https://www.internetvoices.org/press-release/108000/voices-internet-freedom-fcc-chairmans-anti-net-neutrality-plan-will-harm-people) ," Voices for Internet Freedom, April 26, 2017. Harper Neidig, "Poll: 83 percent of voters support keeping FCC's net neutrality rules (http://thehill.com/policy/technology/364528-poll-83-percent-of-voters-support-keeping-fccs-net-neutrality-rules) ," The Hill, Dec. 12, 2017.

(http://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://act.credoaction.com/sign/pass-CRA_dems?source=fb_share4&referring_akid={{user.token}}) Share on Facebook
Post to your wall (http://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://act.credoaction.com/sign/pass-CRA_dems?source=fb_share4&referring_akid={{user.token}}) (https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?text=It's not too late to save %23netneutrality. SIGN THE PETITION telling Congress to overrule the FCC%3A http%3A%2F%2Fshare.credoaction.com%2F373540546t%3Freferring_akid%3D.7170499.vGV096 via %40CREDOMobile) Tweet this
Post to Twitter (https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?text=It%27s%20not%20too%20late%20to%20save%20%23netneutrality.%20SIGN%20THE%20PETITION%20telling%20Congress%20to%20overrule%20the%20FCC%3A%20http%3A%2F%2Fshare.credoaction.com%2F373540546t%3Freferring_akid%3D.7170499.vGV096%20via%20%40CREDOMobile)
If you wish to stop receiving our emails or change your subscription options, please visit this link:
http://demolinks.us/u/OTg0MjAvMC8xOTM2Njk2LzAvMjI3LzAvNmQyYjUzMWYyNDIzMDVlZjBlYTdjNDEyODZkMzM3MWUvMTc2NTEyNTE2

Democrats.com, Box 1452, New York, NY 10276

name: Sen. Jeanne Shaheen (via DSCC.org)
subject: We only need to flip two more seats, friends
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the DSCC, dscc
body:
Doug Jones' win sent a message that momentum is on our side. Now, we only need to flip two more seats to regain the Senate majority, friends. That's why I'm emailing you now:
---
[](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/support-democrats?refcode=DPE-20171226-Shaheen-ProsHV&recurring=auto&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=")
Friends: Doug Jones' win was a huge victory not only for Democrats in Alabama, but nationwide. It sent a message that momentum is on our side, that the resistance has been reignited and that Democrats can take back the Senate next year.

Now, we only need to flip two more seats to regain our majority. But we know that won't be easy: Right-wing groups have already pledged to spend hundreds of millions of dollars to try to buy elections in 2018, so Democrats in tough races across the country can't possibly win without our help.

**That 's why I'm emailing you now, because we need your immediate support to build up the grassroots organizations necessary to take back the Senate next fall. [Please, friends, give $1 right now to the Democratic Senatorial Campaign Committee (DSCC) to help Democrats win in battleground states and take back the Senate.](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/support-democrats?refcode=DPE-20171226-Shaheen-ProsHV&recurring=auto&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=")**

I've had enough of the dangerous, heartless antics of this Republican-controlled Senate. I've had enough of them prioritizing their richest donors' wallets over the needs of hardworking families. The American people deserve better.

We saw during this summer's health care fight just how much every single seat matters. **But Nate Silver reports that Democrats still have a lot to do to take back the Senate** \-- so it's absolutely critical we take advantage of this moment to power the candidates who will help us fight back against Donald Trump and the GOP for years to come.

**Every dollar, every vote will make a difference in these razor-thin races.[Please Friends, give $1 or more right now to the DSCC -- or whatever you can spare -- to help Democrats nationwide win battleground races and take back the Senate.](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/support-democrats?refcode=DPE-20171226-Shaheen-ProsHV&recurring=auto&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=")**

Thank you for fighting by my side.

Senator Jeanne Shaheen

**Democratic Senatorial Campaign Committee
120 Maryland Ave. NE
Washington, DC 20002** Paid for by the DSCC, dscc.org,
and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.
The DSCC is the only committee solely dedicated to supporting Democrats in the U.S. Senate. This message was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com because you are a part of the DSCC's grassroots network. Please add us to your address book or approved senders list to make sure you keep receiving DSCC updates. Emails are a critical way for us to stay in touch with our grassroots supporters. 99% of the DSCC's contributions come from grassroots donors giving less than $200. We also strive to keep you updated on issues related to the Democratic Party's values.

If you'd like to take a break from DSCC emails, [click here](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d0e7/2b07114c/afd0949b/73194476/660921501/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/s/take-an-email-break?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com").

If you'd like to make a contribution to support us, please [click here](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d0e7/2b07114c/afd0949b/73194471/660921501/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/supportdscc?refcode=DPE-disclaimer"). Together, we're going to take back the Senate, but we'll need the help of every single grassroots supporter to do it. If you'd like to stop receiving all emails from the DSCC, please [click here](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d0e7/2b07114c/afd0949b/73194470/660921501/VEsA/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/content/unsubscribe?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com"). We greatly appreciate your continued support. Contributions or gifts to the DSCC are not tax deductible.
---

name: Nina Turner, Our Revolution
subject: $20.18 for 2018
body:
#
Friends,
Do you remember how frustrating it was for progressives following the 2010 midterm elections? Democrats were not prepared for the Tea Party wave that took back the House of Representatives only two years after President Obama's election. Democratic losses exacerbated gerrymandering and caused a frustrating and very challenging final six years to President Obama's time in office, and we are still feeling the effects today. It is time for us to fight back.
[**Will you commit $20.18 to Our Revolution 's organizing efforts each month from now until November 2018? By working together to capitalize on the grassroots momentum, we can build the campaign infrastructure right now that is necessary for a progressive wave in 2018.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7adf1/bed43eb/3ca37eb7/75fe8a85/4024863422/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ourrev-2018?refcode=em171226a&recurring=true")
Our Revolution has more than 500 local groups across the country that are phone banking, canvassing, and holding organizing meetings to take our government back from those billionaires, lobbyists, corporations, and special interests who believe they should have it all at the expense of the majority of us. Your contribution helps us supply our local organizers with training, materials, and assistance coordinating larger direct actions.
We continue to fight back against the establishment's corporate agenda at every turn. Together we will reverse the efforts to take away our health care, raise our taxes to bankroll a tax cut for the top one percent, and divide us by race, gender, class, country of origin, or anything else that they can use as a wedge between us.
[**Pledge your $20.18 monthly recurring contribution to Our Revolution right now and help us start building for a progressive wave in November 2018.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7adf1/bed43eb/3ca37eb7/75fe8a85/4024863422/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ourrev-2018?refcode=em171226a&recurring=true")
We cannot sit on the sidelines while our values are under daily assault by the establishment's agenda. We must continue our progressive push for a better tomorrow. Your action today, along with thousands of others, helps us fight back with a vision for this country that is progressive, inclusive, and intent on returning to a government of, by, and for the many, not the few. Together we can make the difference.
In solidarity,
Nina Turner
President
Our Revolution
**[CONTRIBUTE»](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7adf1/bed43eb/3ca37eb7/75fe8a85/4024863422/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ourrev-2018?refcode=em171226a&recurring=true")**
PAID FOR BY OUR REVOLUTION
603 2ND ST, NE
WASHINGTON, DC, 20002

Not authorized by any candidate or candidate committee. Our Revolution is a 501(c)(4) organization. Donations to Our Revolution are not deductible as charitable contributions for Federal income tax purposes. All donations are made to support Our Revolution's general mission and are not designated for any specific activity.
603 2nd St NE, Washington, DC 20002
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you need to update or change your information or email address, click here to [update your info](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7adf1/bed43eb/3ca37eb7/75fe8a84/4024863422/VEsC/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/information-update?source=email_footer"). Email is one of the most important tools we have to reach supporters like you, but you can let us know if you'd like to [receive fewer emails](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7adf1/bed43eb/3ca37eb7/75fe8a87/4024863422/VEsD/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjIyMTIzIn0=/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/OR-fewer-emails?source=email_footer&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=22123"). We'd hate to see you go, but if you need to do so, click here to [unsubscribe](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7adf1/bed43eb/3ca37eb7/75fe8a86/4024863422/VEsA/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/unsubscribe/"). Help us continue transforming American politics and fighting for progressive candidates and causes by [contributing to Our Revolution here](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7adf1/bed43eb/3ca37eb7/75fe8a81/4024863422/VEsB/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/ourrevolution?refcode=email-footer-OR").
clean_body: Friends, Do you remember how frustrating it was for progressives following the 2010 midterm elections? Democrats were not prepared for the Tea Party wave that took back the House of Representatives only two years after President Obama's election. Democratic losses exacerbated gerrymandering and caused a frustrating and very challenging final six years to President Obama's time in office, and we are still feeling the effects today. It is time for us to fight back. "" Our Revolution has more than 500 local groups across the country that are phone banking, canvassing, and holding organizing meetings to take our government back from those billionaires, lobbyists, corporations, and special interests who believe they should have it all at the expense of the majority of us. Your contribution helps us supply our local organizers with training, materials, and assistance coordinating larger direct actions. We continue to fight back against the establishment's corporate agenda at every turn. Together we will reverse the efforts to take away our health care, raise our taxes to bankroll a tax cut for the top one percent, and divide us by race, gender, class, country of origin, or anything else that they can use as a wedge between us. "" We cannot sit on the sidelines while our values are under daily assault by the establishment's agenda. We must continue our progressive push for a better tomorrow. Your action today, along with thousands of others, helps us fight back with a vision for this country that is progressive, inclusive, and intent on returning to a government of, by, and for the many, not the few. Together we can make the difference. In solidarity, Nina Turner President Our Revolution "" PAID FOR BY OUR REVOLUTION 603 2ND ST, NE WASHINGTON, DC, 20002 Not authorized by any candidate or candidate committee. Our Revolution is a 501(c)(4) organization. Donations to Our Revolution are not deductible as charitable contributions for Federal income tax purposes. All donations are made to support Our Revolution's general mission and are not designated for any specific activity. 603 2nd St NE, Washington, DC 20002

subject: Will you resolve for a RED 2018?
body:

**2017 has been a****phenomenal**** year for the Republican agenda in Concord. **
**In 2017, Republicans in Concord:**
**\- Passed constitutional carry****\- Passed a balanced state budget****\- Lowered business taxes****\- Increased voter integrity ****\- Returned $36.8 million to towns and cities for roads and bridges****\- Cut****unnecessary**** regulations ****\- And much more!**
**Last week Granite Staters received two fantastic presents: tax reform being passed in Washington and Governor Sununu preventing a costly toll increase from hitting hardworking taxpayers.**
**2017 was a great year for New Hampshire Republicans - but we need to make sure 2018 is RED!**

clean_body: Derek, 2017 has been aphenomenal year for the Republican agenda in Concord. In 2017, Republicans in Concord: \- Passed constitutional carry\- Passed a balanced state budget\- Lowered business taxes\- Increased voter integrity \- Returned $36.8 million to towns and cities for roads and bridges\- Cutunnecessary regulations \- And much more! Last week Granite Staters received two fantastic presents: tax reform being passed in Washington and Governor Sununu preventing a costly toll increase from hitting hardworking taxpayers. 2017 was a great year for New Hampshire Republicans - but we need to make sure 2018 is RED! Every investment counts! Sincerely, Jeanie ForresterNH GOP Chair | This email was sent to mailto:dpwillis67@gmail.com Republican Party of New Hampshire 10 Water Street Concord New Hampshire 03301 \--UNSUBSCRIBE_ALT--
